title: Beacon Signal Button
prompt: Create a production-ready Beacon Signal Button using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. It is an original send action that changes from a quiet black label into a clear sent acknowledgement. Place it on a white or #F4F5F6 Bento surface with a 22px radius, #111111 text, #4A4A4F supporting copy, a hairline #ECECEE border, and one cyan beacon accent. Do not use gradients on the card, glossy effects, remote images, or more than two bright accent colors. The resting control has a compact status dot, an arrow glyph, and a readable Send update label. On click, Enter, or Space, swap the label to Sent and expand a short status line beneath it. The dot should make one 480ms opacity-and-scale beacon pulse while the button uses a spring with stiffness 360 and damping 24; use a 160ms ease-out for text opacity. Keep all state local, animate transforms and opacity rather than layout whenever possible, and do not create timers. Use semantic button markup, a visible dark focus ring, an aria-live polite confirmation, at least a 44px target, and a distinct disabled treatment. The compact preview must remain useful at 320px and the detail preview may grow to 360px without page-level fixed positioning. Maintain AA contrast and never communicate completion by color alone. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the final sent state with only a 120ms fade. Return a complete client component plus a pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML.
